title: Velvety Smoky Algerian Soybeans Stir-fry
description: A velvety smoky dish featuring soya beans with a touch of sweet and savory flavors in an easy-to-follow recipe that's sugar-free-friendly.
instructions: Rinse and soak the soybeans overnight. Drain and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a wok or large skillet over medium heat. Add minced garlic, chopped onion, and red pepper flakes; sauté for about 2 minutes until onions are translucent.

Add the soaked soybeans to the skillet. Stir-fry them with salt and sugar-free soy sauce, allowing the flavors to meld together. Continue stirring occasionally until the beans are fully cooked (about 10-15 minutes).
Serve hot as a side dish or on its own as a snack.
Enjoy your cozy Algerian dish that's both smoky and velvety in texture.
